name: Warmian-Masurian Voivodeship (Poland)

colors.symbolism: details of the crown and claws
outlines and details of the eagle

description: Red field with a triangular swallowtail cut at the fly side, bordered along its edges in white, and charged at the hoist with the crowned white eagle of Warmia.
history: Adopted by the Warmian-Masurian Voivodeship after its creation in 1999, drawing on the heraldry of the historic Warmia region.

features.symbolism: A red field with a swallowtail-shaped fly cut, edged in white, charged with the white eagle of Warmia bearing a yellow crown.

features.role: the crowned white eagle of Warmia
features.symbolism: the historic emblem of the Prince-Bishopric of Warmia
